data.title: KKR ramps up Japan real estate bets as asset sales accelerate
data.description:
KKR's (KKR) Japan real estate management subsidiary, KJRM Holdings, plans expansion in purchases of properties that Japanese companies want to sell off—a market the firm estimates at ¥450T, or $2.8T. 

"Corporate demand to sell real estate is very strong due partly to shareholder activists and that will likely be the case for three to five years," according to KJRM Holdings’ president, Naoki Suzuki said in an interview, the Japan Times reported. "The trend of companies selling off their real estate holdings to improve capital efficiency will continue."

The U.S.-based investment firm is positioning its Japan-focused property platform to capture a growing wave of disposals as local corporations face mounting pressure. Suzuki added that more than half of the properties acquired in recent years by KJRM-managed real estate investment trusts and private funds were sourced from corporate divestments.

The Japanese unit is focusing on inflation‑resistant, high‑cash‑flow properties in major cities such as Tokyo, Osaka, and Nagoya, betting that higher rents can offset rising interest rates and borrowing costs.

data.title: Aon raises quarterly dividend by 10.1% to $0.82 a share
data.description:
• Aon (AON) declares $0.82/share quarterly dividend, 10.1% increase from prior dividend of $0.745.
  • Forward yield 1.05%
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record May 1; ex-div May 1.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 10.1% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.745 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See AON Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Lomiko Metals appoints Belinda Labatte as interim CFO
UK homebuilder Vistry appoints Adam Daniels CEO after Greg Fitzgerald steps down
data.description:
• Vistry Group (BVHMY) has appointed Adam Daniels as CEO with immediate effect, after executive chairman Greg Fitzgerald handed in his notice last month, the company said on Monday.
  • The FTSE 250 housebuilder said Daniels, currently executive chair of one of two largest regional divisions, will also join the board as an executive director.
  • Fitzgerald, ⁠who had been ​due to retire as ​chairman at May's annual meeting and remain CEO for up ​to 12 months ​while a successor was found, will ‌step ⁠down from both roles immediately by mutual agreement, the company said.
  • The faster-than-expected transition comes after a difficult period for Vistry (BVHMF), which recently cautioned that margins will stay under pressure into 2026 amid weak demand in the UK housing market.

data.title: AbbVie signs pain drug licensing deal with China’s Haisco
data.description:
• AbbVie (ABBV) has signed an exclusive licensing agreement with China-based Haisco Pharmaceutical Group to advance the global development of novel pain therapies.
  • Under the terms, Haisco grants AbbVie exclusive rights to develop, manufacture, and commercialize multiple pain treatment candidates worldwide, excluding mainland China, Hong Kong, and Macau.
  • The deal covers a portfolio of compounds targeting pain-related indications, currently in development in China across stages ranging from preclinical to Phase 1.
  • Haisco will receive an upfront payment of $30M and is eligible for up to $715M in total development, regulatory, and commercial milestone payments, along with tiered royalties on future net sales.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: China's Ping An plans to offload $1B in North America software funds - report
data.description:
• China’s Ping An Insurance Group (PNGAY) is seeking to sell about $1B of software-focused private equity assets, Bloomberg reported, citing sources.
  • The insurer has hired Campbell Lutyens to run the process, which began in March, as it looks to trim exposure to the sector and generate liquidity.
  • The portfolio being marketed is largely concentrated in North America-focused software funds, including two vehicles managed by Vista Equity Partners dating back to the late 2010s, alongside another fund run by KKR (KKR), the report added.
  • This move comes amid a broader investor appetite cooling for software exposure, with private credit funds increasingly avoiding the sector and several planned exits by private equity firms facing delays.

data.title: Gold Runner Exploration appoints Robert Suttie as CFO; transition plan underway
data.description:
Gold Runner Exploration (GDREF) has appointed Robert Suttie as its new CFO, effective April 1, 2026.

He succeeds Heidi Gutte, who resigned on February 13, 2026. Gutte has transitioned into a consulting role to assist with the company’s year-end filings.

During the interim period between February and April, Chris Wensley, who also serves as a director and the CEO, stepped in as the acting CFO.

Suttie, currently the president of Marrelli Support Services, brings nearly 30 years of financial reporting experience to the role. He  also serves as CFO or director for several TSX Venture Exchange and Canadian Securities Exchange-listed companies.

data.title: Ping An targets $1B exit from software-focused PE assets
data.description:
China’s biggest insurer, Ping An Insurance (PIAIF) shares fell 2.23% in Hong Kong on Monday as the insurer moved to offload a $1 billion portfolio of software-focused private equity assets, according to a Bloomberg report.

The group has reportedly engaged Campbell Lutyens to manage the secondary market sale, a process that reportedly commenced in March.

The portfolio primarily comprises stakes in two software-focused funds managed by Vista Equity Partners and an additional North American fund managed by KKR & Co., the sources said.

Similar to a 2024 transaction, Ping An is expected to offload the assets from its balance sheet while potentially continuing to manage them for new investors, a move designed to boost liquidity and fee-based income, the sources familiar said.

The exit comes as private credit and PE firms begin to cool on the software-as-a-service (SaaS) model, which accounted for nearly half of all PE deal volume in recent years.

As per the report, representatives from Vista, KKR, Campbell Lutyens, and Ping An Insurance Overseas (Holdings) did not comment on this matter.

Despite this strategic divestment, Ping An, holding the largest quota among Chinese insurers, recently gained an additional US$80M Qualified Domestic Institutional Investor ((QDII)) quota in March as part of a regulatory expansion, according to Bloomberg's calculations from State Administration of Foreign Exchange disclosures.

The divestment follows a 4% dip in Ping An’s 2025 investment income, which totaled 154.5 billion yuan. By the end of 2025, its total investment assets were 6.5 trillion yuan. The group's overseas arm currently manages roughly $60 billion in assets.

data.title: Eco (Atlantic) Oil & Gas to farm down 60% offshore Namibia stakes to BP Namibia Energy
data.description:
• Eco (Atlantic) Oil & Gas Ltd. (EOG:CA) has signed an agreement to farm down 60% participating interest in all three of its petroleum exploration licenses offshore Namibia to BP Namibia Energy (BP).
  • The agreement, signed on April 10, will farm out an aggregate of 60% participating interest and transfer operatorship to BP in respect of Block 2012A PEL97 (Cooper license), Blocks 2111B and 2211A PEL99 (Guy license), and Blocks 2211B and 2311A PEL100 (Tamar license).
  • Upon successful completion of the transaction, Eco, through its wholly owned subsidiaries Eco Namibia and Eco Services, will retain a 25% interest in PEL97, PEL99, and PEL100.
  • Completion of the transaction is subject to customary conditions and approvals from the Namibian authorities, the Ministry of Industries, Mines, and Energy, and the Upstream Petroleum Unit at the State House.

data.title: Baker Hughes to sell Waygate Technologies to Hexagon in $1.45B all-cash deal
data.description:
• Baker Hughes (BKR) has entered into an agreement to sell its Waygate Technologies business to Hexagon in an all-cash transaction valued at about $1.45B, the oilfield services firm said on Monday.
  • The sale encompasses Waygate Technologies’ remote visual inspection, ultrasound, radiography, and imaging solutions portfolios, and all assets of the business, including intellectual property, footprint and resources.
  • The divestiture, along with three completed deals and the pending purchase of Chart Industries, shows Baker Hughes’ focus on reshaping its portfolio. These actions are aimed at making earnings and cash flow more stable while also strengthening its balance sheet, the company said.
  • The deal is expected to close in the second half of 2026. J.P. Morgan Securities LLC is serving as exclusive financial advisor for Baker Hughes on this transaction.
